inputs.userGoal: Create a real-time document Q&A API leveraging Azure OpenAI and Azure Cosmos DB, expose endpoints using Azure API Management, and enforce rate limits per user.

architecturePrinciples: Secure by default — managed identity for service-to-service auth, no API keys in code
Cost-efficient — serverless Cosmos DB and Consumption-tier APIM to minimize idle costs
Observable — Application Insights and Log Analytics for end-to-end tracing
Rate-limited — APIM rate-limit-by-key policy enforces per-user throttling

reasoning.whyChosen: User-assigned managed identity enables secure, keyless authentication from the App Service to Azure OpenAI and Cosmos DB, aligning with the Security pillar of the Well-Architected Framework.
alternativesConsidered: System-assigned managed identity — less portable across resources
API keys stored in Key Vault — additional secret rotation overhead
reasoning.tradeoffs: Requires explicit role assignments per target resource but provides a single reusable identity for all services.

reasoning.whyChosen: Centralized log sink for Application Insights and APIM diagnostics. PerGB2018 is the recommended default for new workspaces.
alternativesConsidered: Free tier — 500 MB/day cap is too restrictive for production
CapacityReservation — overkill for initial workload
reasoning.tradeoffs: Pay-as-you-go pricing scales linearly; switch to CapacityReservation at 100+ GB/day.

resources.name: appi-docqa-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Workspace-based Application Insights provides end-to-end distributed tracing, request metrics, and failure analysis for the Q&A API and APIM gateway.
alternativesConsidered: Classic Application Insights — deprecated in favor of workspace-based
reasoning.tradeoffs: Pricing governed by Log Analytics ingestion; sampling can reduce cost at the expense of trace completeness.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Application Insights requires a Log Analytics workspace as its data store.

resources.name: oai-docqa-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Azure OpenAI provides enterprise-grade GPT models with data privacy guarantees. S0 is the standard paid tier required for production workloads. East US has broad model availability.
alternativesConsidered: Azure AI Foundry (AIServices kind) — more than needed for a single-model Q&A scenario
Self-hosted open-source LLM on AKS — higher operational burden
reasoning.tradeoffs: S0 has per-token pricing; high-volume usage may benefit from provisioned throughput (PTU) commitments.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Managed identity needs to be created first so it can be assigned the Cognitive Services OpenAI User role.

resources.name: oai-docqa-prod-001/gpt-4o

reasoning.whyChosen: GPT-4o is the latest multimodal model offering strong Q&A performance with lower latency and cost compared to GPT-4 Turbo.
alternativesConsidered: gpt-4 — higher cost per token, single modality
gpt-35-turbo — lower quality for complex document Q&A
reasoning.tradeoffs: Standard (pay-per-token) deployment; switch to Provisioned for predictable throughput at scale.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Model deployment is a child resource of the Azure OpenAI account.

resources.name: cosmos-docqa-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Cosmos DB NoSQL API with serverless capacity provides low-latency document storage and retrieval with automatic indexing. Serverless eliminates idle throughput cost for variable workloads. Session consistency offers read-your-own-writes guarantees ideal for user-facing Q&A.
alternativesConsidered: Azure SQL Database — less flexible schema for document-centric data
Cosmos DB with provisioned throughput — better for sustained high-RU workloads but higher baseline cost
Azure Blob Storage — no built-in query engine
reasoning.tradeoffs: Serverless is single-region only and cannot combine with multi-region writes or analytical store. Suitable for initial scale; migrate to provisioned throughput if usage exceeds serverless limits (1 TB storage, 5000 RU/s burst).

resources.dependencyReasoning: Managed identity needs to be created first for Cosmos DB RBAC data-plane access.

reasoning.whyChosen: App Service Plan B1 (Linux) provides a cost-effective compute layer for the Q&A API backend with always-on support. Linux reduces licensing costs compared to Windows.
alternativesConsidered: S1 Standard — adds autoscale and deployment slots but higher cost for initial deployment
Azure Container Apps — serverless containers, but adds complexity for a single API
Azure Functions — event-driven model less suited to long-running HTTP completions from OpenAI
reasoning.tradeoffs: B1 lacks autoscale and deployment slots; upgrade to S1 or P0v3 when traffic warrants it.

resources.name: app-docqa-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: App Service hosts the Q&A API backend that orchestrates calls to Azure OpenAI and Cosmos DB. Python 3.12 is ideal for LLM-based applications with rich SDK support (openai, azure-cosmos). HTTPS-only and TLS 1.2 enforce secure transport.
alternativesConsidered: Azure Container Apps — viable but adds container build/push complexity
Azure Functions — timeout constraints for streaming OpenAI responses
reasoning.tradeoffs: Tightly coupled to App Service Plan scaling; container-based deployment offers more portability.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires the App Service Plan for compute, managed identity for auth, OpenAI and Cosmos DB as backends, and Application Insights for telemetry.

resources.name: apim-docqa-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: API Management Consumption tier provides serverless API gateway with built-in rate-limit-by-key policy for per-user throttling, subscription key management, and request/response transformation — all with zero idle cost. The rate-limit-by-key policy can key on subscription ID, JWT claim, or IP address to enforce per-user limits.
alternativesConsidered: Developer tier — adds developer portal and VNet support but has hourly cost even when idle
StandardV2 — production-grade with VNet integration but significantly higher baseline cost
Azure Front Door + custom rate limiting — more complex to configure per-user policies
reasoning.tradeoffs: Consumption tier has a cold-start latency (~1-2s on first request), no built-in developer portal, no VNet support, and a 10,000 requests/min gateway limit. Upgrade to BasicV2 or StandardV2 for higher throughput or VNet needs.

resources.dependencyReasoning: APIM routes traffic to the App Service backend and sends diagnostics to Application Insights.

overallReasoning.summary: This architecture pairs Azure OpenAI (GPT-4o) with Cosmos DB (NoSQL, serverless) behind an App Service API, fronted by API Management for secure, rate-limited access. A user-assigned managed identity provides keyless authentication across all services. Application Insights with Log Analytics delivers full observability. The Consumption tier for APIM and serverless Cosmos DB minimize costs during low-traffic periods while scaling on demand.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: Serverless Cosmos DB and Consumption APIM optimize for cost but introduce cold-start latency and impose throughput ceilings. The B1 App Service Plan lacks autoscale — upgrade to Standard or Premium when traffic grows. No VNet isolation in this default plan; add Private Endpoints and VNet integration for enterprise-grade network security.

plan.validation: All resources deploy to East US. Cosmos DB serverless is single-region (matching the single-location config). APIM Consumption requires no VNet (set to None). App Service B1 supports always-on. Managed identity enables RBAC-based access to OpenAI (Cognitive Services OpenAI User role) and Cosmos DB (Cosmos DB Built-in Data Contributor role). No SKU or subnet conflicts detected.

references.title: RAG pattern with Azure OpenAI and Cosmos DB
